turns.content: Show me an example of the observer pattern with events

turns.thinking: The user wants to see the observer pattern using pi events. I'll show a real-world example with multiple extensions observing build state.

turns.content:
## How It Works

```
build:start event
       в”‚
       в”њв”Ђв”Ђв–є UI Notifier в”Ђв”Ђв–є Shows modal
       в”‚
       в””в”Ђв”Ђв–є Logger в”Ђв”Ђв–є Records log

build:phase event
       в”‚
       в”њв”Ђв”Ђв–є UI Notifier в”Ђв”Ђв–є Updates progress
       в”‚
       в””в”Ђв”Ђв–є Logger в”Ђв”Ђв–є Records phase
```

**Key insight:** Build runner doesn't know about UI or LoggerвЂ”it just emits events. Extensions react independently. This is **decoupled** design.

<Callout variant="tip" title="Benefits">
- **Add new observers** without modifying the emitter
- **Remove observers** without breaking the emitter
- **Test each part** independently
- **Extend functionality** by adding more observers
</Callout>
